Name: Students for the Exploration and Development of Space (SEDS)
Relevance: Designs and hot-fire tests liquid bi-propellant rocket engines like SPARK I and competes in the Spaceport America Cup.

Name: RebelSat
Relevance: Student-led initiative dedicated to designing, building, testing, and operating CubeSats for space-based research.

Name: Nevada Center for Astrophysics (NCfA)
Relevance: Advances theoretical and observational research on black holes, exoplanets, and cosmology utilizing large-scale simulations.

Name: American Institute of Aeronautics and Astronautics (AIAA)
Relevance: Fosters interest and implements aerospace-related engineering projects, aeronautics, and space applications for students.

Name: Department of Physics and Astronomy
Relevance: Manages the university's core space science curriculum, observational labs, and conducts astrophysical research.

Name: Unmanned Aerial Systems (UAS) Program
Relevance: Interdisciplinary team focusing on UAS technology, unmanned aerial vehicle testing, and the autonomous aviation industry.

Name: Drones and Autonomous Systems Lab (DASL) / Robotics Lab
Relevance: Develops unmanned aerial systems and humanoid robots with applications directly transferable to aerospace automation.

Name: High Pressure Science and Engineering Center (HiPSEC)
Relevance: Studies matter under extreme high pressure and temperature conditions, crucial for understanding planetary interiors and space material survivability.

Name: GIS and Remote Sensing Lab
Relevance: Conducts research in geographic information systems and remote sensing, which are foundational technologies for satellite-based earth observation.

Name: Laser-Plasma Physics Group
Relevance: Conducts research on charge transfer processes and radiative lifetimes in tokamak fusion plasmas, which has theoretical overlap with advanced space propulsion.

Name: Introductory Astronomy Laboratory (astlab)
Relevance: Provides hands-on observation with Schmidt-Cassegrain telescopes, but functions primarily as an instructional facility rather than a distinct research group.
